member.name: Graham Stringer
member.role: MP for Blackley and Middleton South

addresses: North Manchester Sixth Form College, Rochdale Road, Manchester, M9 4AF

member.synopsis: Graham Stringer is the Labour MP for Blackley and Middleton South , and has been an MP continually since 1 May 1997.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Minimum Wage: Blackley and Middleton South
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what estimate he has made of the number of young workers in Blackley and Middleton South who will be effected by (a) the delay in aligning the Minimum Wage with other workers and (b) the total estimate
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data on the effects of the National Minimum Wage at constituent level is not held. However, in the North West an estimated 30,000 on the 18–20 rate are expected to benefit from the 8.5% increase of the 18-20-year-old National Minimum Wage on 1 April.The g...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Insulation: Housing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what estimate the Government has made of the workforce that will be required each year on average to retrofit insulation to UK housing stock by 2050 as required in the Government's Net Zero c
writtenQuestions.answerText: Analysis carried out by the Construction Industry Training Board in 2021 estimated that the pathway to decarbonising buildings by 2050 would require around 12,000 workers a year to be trained in retrofit skills over the following four years, rising to 30,...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Carbon Emissions: Engineering
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what estimate the Government has made of the number of qualified electrical engineers required to deliver Net Zero by 2050.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The electricity network will be a key enabler of a secure, resilient, net zero energy system. The Electricity Networks Strategic Framework published in August 2022 suggests onshore network investment to meet net zero could support 50,000-130,000 FTE jobs ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Electricity Generation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, with reference to the Gibson and Aris report entitled The Future of GB Electricity Supply: Security, Cost and Emissions, what assessment he has made of the implications for his policies of th
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has launched the Review of Electricity Market Arrangements (REMA), which aims to create the market arrangements to deliver the investment needed to decarbonise and find the most cost-effective route to a net zero power sector by 2035. It al...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Moderna: Contracts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what recent assessment she has made of the potential effect of developing a UK vaccine research and manufacturing centre with Moderna on preparedness for future pandemics.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In June 2022, the Government agreed heads of terms with Moderna to create a strategic partnership over the next decade. A full contract is being negotiated with Moderna, subject to approval of a full business case.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Moderna: Contracts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what progress her Department has made on agreeing a contract with Moderna to develop a centre for vaccine research and manufacture in the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In June 2022, the Government agreed heads of terms with Moderna to create a strategic partnership over the next decade. A full contract is being negotiated with Moderna, subject to approval of a full business case.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: High Speed 2 Line: Greater Manchester
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, when he plans to place in the Library the study commissioned by HS2 on a like-for-like comparison on the surface station and the underground station for HS2 at Manchester Piccadilly.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Manchester Piccadilly High Speed Station – Design of an Alternative Underground Station was placed in the Libraries of both Houses on 27 June. The report is also publicly available on the HS2 Ltd website.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Electricity: Storage
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy, what estimate his Department has made of the seasonal electricity storage capacity that will be required to deliver the net zero target.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Longer duration storage, including seasonal electricity storage, can help manage variation in generation and demand over long periods of time by storing excess generation until times when generation sources are less available or demand is greater. Analysi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy

writtenQuestions.heading: Electricity: Storage
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy, what estimate his Department has made of the capital cost of seasonal electricity storage capacity.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are a range of technologies that could provide longer duration storage, including seasonal electricity storage. BEIS has published technical data and cost projections for electricity storage technologies that could be commercially deployed in the fu...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy

writtenQuestions.heading: Crime: Victim Support Schemes
writtenQuestions.questionText: How many meetings she has had with the Mayor of Greater Manchester since the recent publication of Her Majesty's Inspectorate of Constabulary and Fire & Rescue Services' report, An inspection of the service provided to victims of crime by Greater Manchest
writtenQuestions.answerText: It is the Mayor’s responsibility to ensure Greater Manchester Police act immediately and effectively to provide victims of crime, especially the vulnerable, with the high-quality service they deserve.The Policing Minister met the Deputy Mayor and acting C...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Contracts: Payments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy, whether it is permissible under the Contracts for Difference regime for top-up payments to be made to third parties outside the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Low Carbon Contracts Company (LCCC) pays electricity generators that have signed a Contract for Difference (CfD) a flat, indexed, rate for the electricity that they produce over a 15-year period. The amount paid is the difference between the ‘strike p...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy

writtenQuestions.heading: Contracts: Tax Avoidance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy, what steps his Department is taking to prevent tax avoidance by participants in the Contracts for Difference scheme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Contracts for Difference (CfD) scheme is the Government’s main mechanism for supporting low carbon electricity generation. The Low Carbon Contracts Company (LCCC) works with CfD generators to ensure that they deliver on their contractual commitments t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy

writtenQuestions.heading: Contracts: Payments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy, whether top-up payments to be made to third parties are permissible, under the Contracts for Difference regime.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Low Carbon Contracts Company (LCCC) pays electricity generators that have signed a Contract for Difference (CfD) a flat, indexed, rate for the electricity that they produce over a 15-year period. The amount paid is the difference between the ‘strike p...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy

writtenQuestions.heading: Wind Power: Seas and Oceans
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy, with reference to his Department's BEIS Electricity Generation Costs (2020) published on 24 August 2020, what estimated value for the annual decline in performance of offshore win
writtenQuestions.answerText: BEIS estimates a constant load factor and availability across the lifetime of an offshore wind farm, and therefore a constant performance. This is based on research performed by Arup (2016)[1] and independently peer-reviewed by Professor Derek Bunn (2020)...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy

writtenQuestions.heading: Wind Power
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy, with reference to his Department's BEIS Electricity Generation Costs (2020) published on 24 August 2020, if he will publish the empirical evidence, other than bid prices, underlyi
writtenQuestions.answerText: The estimates of (a) falling capital and (b) operational costs for (i) onshore and (ii) offshore wind generation are informed by a variety of internal and external evidence sources. These include published estimates from Arup (2016)[1] and DNV GL (2019)[2...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy

writtenQuestions.heading: Electricity Generation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy, with reference to his Department's BEIS Electricity Generation Costs (2020) published on 24 August 2020, if he will publish the empirical, numerical evidence underlying its estima
writtenQuestions.answerText: The evidence behind the (a) capital and (b) operational cost assumptions for (i) Combined Cycle Gas Turbines and (ii) other conventional generation technologies has been published in a report prepared by Leigh Fisher and Jacobs (2016)[1]. Minor updates to...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy

writtenQuestions.heading: Gambling: Coronavirus
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Digital, Culture, Media and Sport, pursuant to the Answer of 8 December 2020 to Question 125212 on Gambling: Coronavirus, if he will make an assessment of the potential merits of allowing adult gaming centres to open unde
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government, with advice from SAGE, reviewed the impact of the previous tiering arrangements and decided that unfortunately stricter rules on tier 3 closures would be necessary to have an impact on the rate of transmission in very high alert areas. Thi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Digital, Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Gambling: Coronavirus
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Digital, Culture, Media and Sport, pursuant to the Answer of 8 December 2020 to Question 125212 on Gambling: Coronavirus, what plans the Government has to review restrictions on adult gaming centres in covid-19 tier three
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government, with advice from SAGE, reviewed the impact of the previous tiering arrangements and decided that unfortunately stricter rules on tier 3 closures would be necessary to have an impact on the rate of transmission in very high alert areas. Thi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Digital, Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Solar Power: Planning Permission
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy, what estimate he has made of agricultural land in each Agricultural Land Classification (ACL) category 1 to 5 which is in scoping or pre-application or is awaiting decision on an
writtenQuestions.answerText: We have not made any estimate of agricultural land in Agricultural Land Classification categories, which is in the planning system or has been granted consent for development as solar photovoltaic generation, and we do not hold this information.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy

writtenQuestions.heading: Solar Power: Planning Permission
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy, what estimate he has made of agricultural land in each Agricultural Land Classification (ACL) category 1 to 5 which has been granted planning consent for development as solar phot
writtenQuestions.answerText: We have not made any estimate of agricultural land in Agricultural Land Classification categories, which is in the planning system or has been granted consent for development as solar photovoltaic generation, and we do not hold this information.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business, Energy and Industrial Strategy
